summary: Phase 4.0 (planning gate for the Procedural Atlas Generator) complete. The decision memo at artifacts/decision-memo-phase-4.md exists, was reviewed by architecture-red-team (artifacts/red-team-phase-4.md, initial verdict NEEDS REWORK with 8 blockers B1-B8 and 17 nits N1-N17), all 8 blockers were resolved via an in-memo addendum that supersedes the original prose where they conflict (decision-memo-phase-4.md:2026-3084), and a follow-up architecture-red-team review of the addendum confirmed blocker resolution with verdict APPROVE WITH NITS (8/8 resolved, 17/17 nit dispositions accepted, 3 minor new nits N18-N20 raised as 4.A.1/4.A.2 carry-forwards). The memo locks in 14 numbered decisions covering the recipe primitive set, recipe registration contract, atlas layout strategy, deterministic indexed-PNG encoding, color palette, atlas JSON manifest, rulesetVersion derivation, atlas-seed selection, dev-mode preview UI, build script wiring, layer-import additions, test surfaces, the Phase 4.0/4.A.1/4.A.2/4.B phase split (mirroring Phase 3's pattern), and deferred work. Per addendum B1, the placeholder ruleset retirement is folded into Phase 4.A.2 (atomic with the atlas binary itself) so no transient sentinel ever ships on master; per B2, rulesetTextHash now hashes the alphabetically-sorted (utf8_path, NUL, sha256(normalize(content)), NUL) tuples so file renames bump the hash; per B5, atlasBinaryHash is computed in a vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash plugin with a deterministic EMPTY_SHA256 fallback when assets/atlas.png is missing; per B7, atlasSeedToBytes uses a distinct 'icefall:atlas-seed:v1:' domain anchor collision-free with seedToBytes. docs/PHASES.md and docs/ATLAS_GENERATOR.md were updated alongside the memo to reflect the split and the steady-state design; docs/ARCHITECTURE.md was deliberately NOT touched (deferred to 4.A.1's drift-detection sweep per the Phase 3.A.1 precedent). No net-new src/atlas/** code was written — Phase 4.0 is planning only. Phase 4.A.1 (drift-detection sweep) is the next phase and will land the uniformIndex relocation, fdeflate exact-pinned devDependency, .gitattributes, vite-plugin scaffolding with EMPTY_SHA256 fallback, deriveRulesetVersion helper defined-but-unused, rules-text BOM/CRLF scan test, and ARCHITECTURE.md update.
suggested_commit_message: phase-4.0: planning gate (decision memo + red-team + addendum resolving B1-B8)

verification_sprint.purpose: Phase 4.0 is a planning gate (no net-new src/** code). The verification sprint confirms the docs-only changes (decision memo, red-team review, PHASES.md, ATLAS_GENERATOR.md) did not introduce regressions in the existing toolchain or test suite.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Not re-run for Phase 4.0; HEAD of phase-3.B was 563 passing (vitest run --coverage), 35 test files, with 100% line/statement/function coverage on src/core/**, src/mapgen/**, src/registries/** and src/sim/** at 97.94/91.94/100/97.94 above the documented-deviation thresholds. Phase 4.0 changes are docs-only (4 files: 2 new artifacts + 2 doc updates), so no source-coverage delta.
verification_sprint.lint: Not re-run for Phase 4.0; HEAD of phase-3.B was 0 errors. No source files modified.
verification_sprint.typecheck: Not re-run for Phase 4.0; HEAD of phase-3.B was 0 errors. No TS source files modified.
verification_sprint.build: Not re-run for Phase 4.0; HEAD of phase-3.B's locally-rebuilt bundle was 38.91 KB raw / 15.06 KB gzipped (filename index-Bx1CgJqA.js, byte-identical to live deploy).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Phase 4.0 produces no user-visible changes; the live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ continues to serve the Phase 3.A.2 diagnostic page exactly as approved at the HEAD of phase-3.B. The Phase 4 atlas-preview UI lands in 4.A.2 (sandbox) and is verified live in 4.B.

decision_memo.evidence: artifacts/decision-memo-phase-4.md is 3,084 lines and resolves 14 numbered decisions covering: (1) recipe primitive set — 10 orthogonal pure primitives (paletteIndex, paletteSwap, paletteGradient, bayerThreshold, valueNoise2D, rectMask, circleMask, lineMask, columnShift, scanlineResidue) with Floyd-Steinberg / Perlin / polygon / paint-bucket explicitly deferred; (2) recipe signature `(prng, palette, ctx) => Uint8Array` of length tileSize² with stable IDs `atlas-recipe.<theme>.<category>.<name>`; (3) fixed 16×16 tile grid, 1-pixel transparent padding, 16 wide × 8 high (272×136 px), no bin-packing; (4) custom minimal indexed-PNG writer using fdeflate level 1 + filter type 0 + canonical chunk order IHDR/PLTE/tRNS/IDAT/IEND + zero ancillary chunks; (5) indexed-PNG color type 3, 16-color cyberpunk-neon-v1 palette, entry 0 transparent, entries 1-15 fully opaque (no partial alpha); (6) canonical-JSON manifest with alphabetically-sorted keys, strict-parse semantics mirroring parseFloor, schemaVersion=1, tile-grid coordinates; (7) rulesetVersion = sha256(rulesetTextHash ‖ '|' ‖ atlasBinaryHash) with the rules file list pinned; (8) Phase 4 placeholder atlas seed 'icefall-phase4-placeholder-atlas-seed', frozen-with-review post-Phase-9; (9) free-form text-input seed + 4 hardcoded preset-seed buttons + side-by-side build-vs-live <canvas> comparison with window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_* flags; (10) `npm run gen-atlas` (vite-node) + CI gate `git diff --exit-code assets/`; (11) new src/atlas/** layer with no-float / no-time / no-async / no imports from sim/mapgen/render/input/main, ARCHITECTURE.md update deferred to 4.A.1's drift sweep; (12) per-primitive unit goldens + per-recipe goldens + whole-atlas ATLAS_DIGEST self-test + cross-OS byte-equality matrix + parseAtlasJson + atlas-stream-isolation self-tests; (13) phase split into 4.0 / 4.A.1 / 4.A.2 / 4.B mirroring Phase 3's pattern; (14) deferred: full sprite content, Floyd-Steinberg/Perlin/polygon-fill/paint-bucket, CRT shader, theme switching, mod loader, animated sprites, audio.

red_team_review.evidence: artifacts/red-team-phase-4.md is 1,088 lines. The initial review (verdict NEEDS REWORK BLOCKERS) raised 8 blockers (B1: phase split contradiction; B2: rulesText file-list not in hash; B3: .gitattributes missing; B4: in-browser regen byte-equality unpinned; B5: atlasBinaryHash in vite.config.ts top-level chicken-and-egg; B6: tile-grid bumps additive vs binary stability; B7: atlasSeed shares seedToBytes with no domain anchor; B8: atlas-stream-isolation false-exclude on simFloor) and 17 nits (N1-N17 covering encoder pin/upgrade procedure, fdeflate cross-runtime claim citation, valueNoise2D PRNG advancement, paletteGradient div-by-zero, palette-index bounds check, tRNS truncation policy, DEV- error message fixture, bundle budget, atlas-grid wrap-with-skip edge case, node: import-prefix pin, theme regex, variant-A golden hex pin-source, modding-build-time-vs-runtime, cross-OS Node version + fail-fast, manifest parse/serialize asymmetry, animation-frames forward-compat, define-block JSON-stringify foot-gun).

addendum_resolving_blockers.evidence: artifacts/decision-memo-phase-4.md:2026-3084 (~1058 lines of addendum). Each of B1-B8 has a numbered resolution with a byte-explicit fix, a regression test, and 'Decisions amended above' callouts. Key resolutions: B1 placeholder retirement folded into 4.A.2 atomically (4.A.1 helper defined-but-unused, no transient sentinel on master); B2 rulesetTextHash rewritten as alphabetically-sorted (utf8_path, NUL, sha256(normalizeForHash(content)), NUL) tuples with CRLF→LF + BOM stripping pinned; B3 byte-exact .gitattributes content pinned as a 4.A.1 deliverable plus tests/build/rules-text.test.ts CRLF/BOM scan with pinned error messages; B4 encoder pinned to Uint8Array + @noble/hashes/sha256 only with atlas-encoder-cross-runtime self-test; B5 atlasBinaryHash moved to vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash with EMPTY_SHA256 fallback and __ATLAS_MISSING__ flag; B6 tile-grid resize allowed only at rulesetVersion boundaries with red-team review and tests/atlas/layout-constants.test.ts trip-wire; B7 new atlasSeedToBytes(seed) = sha256(utf8('icefall:atlas-seed:v1:') ‖ utf8(seed)) with byte-distinctness regression test; B8 atlas-stream-isolation rewritten as __consumed.size === 1. The 17 nits are dispositioned as 11 resolved-in-addendum (N1, N2, N3, N4, N5, N6, N7, N10, N11, N14, N17) / 4 carried-to-4.A.2-implementation (N8, N9, N13, N15) / 0 carried-to-4.A.1 (B3 absorbed all 4.A.1-scoped nit work) / 1 deferred-to-later-phase (N16 — animation schemaVersion=2 migrator deferred to Phase 9). N12 is hybrid: the rule for computing variant goldens is pinned in the addendum; the literal hex values are pinned during 4.A.2.

follow_up_red_team_review.evidence: Per docs/PHASES.md acceptance criteria for planning-gate phases (mirroring Phase 3.0's 'A follow-up architecture-red-team review of the addendum confirms blocker resolution' criterion), the same architecture-red-team agent re-reviewed the addendum and appended a 'Follow-up review (post-addendum)' section to artifacts/red-team-phase-4.md. Verdict: APPROVE WITH NITS. Disposition: 8 resolved / 0 partial / 0 unresolved out of 8 blockers; 17 accepted / 0 rejected out of 17 nits. Three new minor nits raised in the follow-up review (N18-N20): N18 = 4.A.1's tests/build/rules-text.test.ts will ENOENT on three RULES_FILES entries that don't land until 4.A.2 — fold into 4.A.1 by either marking those entries as 4.A.2-pending or by writing the test to skip-when-absent; N19 = B7's validateSeedString is described as a Phase 2 frozen contract that does not actually exist in src/core/seed.ts — atlas-seed validation is a Phase 4 addition and should be implemented in 4.A.1; N20 = B8's per-call delta template prose is loose because __consumed is a Set (duplicate adds are no-ops) — the rewritten self-test itself is correct. All three are small carry-forwards into 4.A.1/4.A.2 implementation; none warrants a re-review.

phase_split_documented.evidence: docs/PHASES.md Phase 4 section now opens with a callout block (lines 191-224) mirroring Phase 3's lines 131-148: Phase 4.0 = planning gate (this approval), Phase 4.A.1 = drift-detection sweep (uniformIndex relocation to src/core/prng.ts, fdeflate exact-pinned devDependency, .gitattributes with assets/atlas.png binary + LF normalization, tests/build/rules-text.test.ts CRLF/BOM scan, vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash scaffolding with EMPTY_SHA256 fallback, deriveRulesetVersion helper defined-but-unused, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md update referencing Phase 4 frozen contracts and the new atlasSeedToBytes domain anchor — no atlas implementation, no placeholder retirement, no __RULESET_VERSION__ call-site flip), Phase 4.A.2 = sandbox-verifiable atlas implementation (primitives, recipes, registry, encoder, manifest, generator, preview UI, ATLAS_DIGEST self-test, the atomic call-site flip from PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION to deriveRulesetVersion alongside assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json), and Phase 4.B = live-deploy verification + cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on ubuntu-latest / macos-latest / windows-latest with fail-fast: false and node-version: 20.x. Phase 4.0 acceptance criteria, Phase 4.A.1 acceptance criteria, Phase 4.A.2 acceptance criteria, and Phase 4.B acceptance criteria are partitioned across docs/PHASES.md:244-275.

atlas_generator_doc_updated.evidence: docs/ATLAS_GENERATOR.md (274 lines, was 26 lines of placeholder) is rewritten as a steady-state design doc summarizing the 13 frozen contracts (the 14th decision is the phase split, which lives in docs/PHASES.md). The doc points back to artifacts/decision-memo-phase-4.md for full rationale and addendum amendments. Header status note flipped from 'placeholder' to 'design doc' with a pointer to the addendum for any superseded prose.

architecture_doc_deferred_to_4_A_1.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md was deliberately NOT modified by Phase 4.0. Per memo decision 11 and the Phase 3.A.1 precedent (drift-detection sweep landing the ARCHITECTURE update for Phase 3 frozen contracts alongside the Phase 2.A code-review carry-forwards), Phase 4.A.1's drift-detection sweep will add: the new src/atlas/** layer-table entry including src/atlas/seed.ts and atlasSeedToBytes per addendum B7, the recipe primitive signatures, the streams.atlas(recipeId) accessor with per-call __consumed.size += 1 invariant, atlas layout constants with the tile-grid resize rule per addendum B6, the PNG encoder format with the all-16-byte tRNS chunk and per-pixel palette-bounds check per addendum N5/N6, the rulesetVersion derivation per addendum B2, the atlas-loader DEV- refusal + hash check with pinned error strings per addendum N7, the ATLAS_DIGEST golden constant, the new ESLint scope additions per addendum B4 (no node:buffer / crypto / node:crypto / crypto.subtle / Buffer in src/atlas/**) and per addendum N10 (bare-fs/path ban for tools/**), and the deferred contracts.

verification.code_review_status: Phase 4.0 produced no application code; only the decision memo, the red-team review, and two doc updates. No code-reviewer invocation required (no source files to review).
verification.qa_playwright_status: Phase 4.0 produced no user-visible changes; the live URL still serves the Phase 3.A.2 diagnostic page approved at the HEAD of phase-3.B. The Phase 4 atlas-preview UI is a Phase 4.A.2 deliverable and will be verified by the qa-playwright equivalent (CI Playwright job) in Phase 4.B.

follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_4_A_: Drift-detection sweep deliverables per docs/PHASES.md:248-257: uniformIndex relocation from src/sim/run.ts to src/core/prng.ts with focused unit tests (termination, unbiased distribution, n=1 boundary); fdeflate exact-pinned devDependency added to package.json; .gitattributes created with the byte-exact content pinned in addendum B3; tests/build/rules-text.test.ts added asserting LF endings and no UTF-8 BOM on every RULES_FILES entry with pinned error-message format; vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash scaffolding added in scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs and wired into vite.config.ts and vitest.config.ts with the EMPTY_SHA256 fallback and __ATLAS_MISSING__ flag; deriveRulesetVersion(rulesText, atlasBinaryHash) helper exported from src/build-info.ts but defined-but-not-yet-called per addendum B1 (define-block call sites continue to inject PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION); docs/ARCHITECTURE.md updated to reference Phase 4 frozen contracts and the new src/atlas/** layer including src/atlas/seed.ts with atlasSeedToBytes per addendum B7.

Follow-up red-team N18: tests/build/rules-text.test.ts will ENOENT on three RULES_FILES entries (src/atlas/palette.ts, src/atlas/params.ts, src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts) that don't land until 4.A.2. Fold by either marking those entries as 4.A.2-pending in RULES_FILES or by writing the test to skip-when-absent. The 4.A.2 step that creates those files will then drop the skip and the test starts asserting against them.

Follow-up red-team N19: B7 introduces validateSeedString as a precondition for atlasSeedToBytes. The addendum text describes it as a Phase 2 frozen contract that already exists, but src/core/seed.ts does not currently export validateSeedString. Atlas-seed validation is a Phase 4 addition and should be implemented in 4.A.1's drift sweep alongside atlasSeedToBytes.

Sandbox-verifiable atlas implementation per docs/PHASES.md:259-268: src/atlas/primitives/** (10 primitives per memo decision 1), src/atlas/recipes/** (7 recipes per Phase 4 deliverables in docs/PHASES.md:242), src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts, src/atlas/encoder.ts (custom minimal indexed-PNG writer), src/atlas/manifest.ts (parseAtlasJson + serializeAtlasJson), tools/gen-atlas.ts, npm run gen-atlas script with CI gate `git diff --exit-code assets/`, src/atlas/preview.ts (preview UI module), atlas preview section in src/main.ts, the atomic vite.config.ts + vitest.config.ts define-block flip from PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION to deriveRulesetVersion(rulesText, atlasBinaryHash), assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json, ATLAS_DIGEST golden constant + sim-cross-runtime-style cross-runtime self-tests (atlas-stream-isolation per addendum B8 using __consumed.size === 1, atlas-manifest-parse, atlas-encoder-cross-runtime per addendum B4).

Memo nits carried to 4.A.2 implementation: N8 (75 KB JS bundle budget impact) — verify the new src/atlas/** preview UI fits under the 75 KB JS gzip cap; N9 (atlas-grid wrap-with-skip edge case) — fixture test for recipes whose tile coords would wrap past the grid edge; N13 (modding-build-time-vs-runtime semantics) — pin in 4.A.2 docstring on the recipe registry; N15 (manifest parse/serialize asymmetry) — round-trip test on non-canonical inputs; N12 (preset-variant golden hex values) — compute the four hex values during 4.A.2 on the ubuntu-latest shard and paste literally into src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts.

Follow-up red-team N20: B8's prose on per-call delta templates is loose because __consumed is a Set (duplicate adds are no-ops). The rewritten self-test itself is correct (asserts __consumed.size === 1 per atlas-generator code path); only the generalizing template prose needs tightening when 4.A.2 writes the analogous tests for the other code paths. Tighten the prose in the implementation PR's test-file comment.
follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_4_B: Live-deploy verification per docs/PHASES.md:270-275: live GitHub Pages URL serves the updated diagnostic page with the in-browser atlas-preview section working; cross-runtime Playwright job exercises the atlas-preview section on chromium / firefox / webkit asserting window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW__ === 'ready', window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_BUILD_HASH__ === window.__ATLAS_PREVIEW_LIVE_HASH__ on ATLAS_SEED_DEFAULT, and each of the four preset seeds (placeholder, variant-A, variant-B, variant-C) regenerates to its pinned expectedHash per addendum N12; new cross-os-atlas-equality GitHub Actions job (matrix fail-fast: false, node-version: 20.x per addendum N14) on ubuntu-latest / macos-latest / windows-latest asserts pairwise SHA-256 equality of generated assets/atlas.png; the four preset-seed expectedHash values are computed during 4.A.2 on the ubuntu-latest shard and pasted into src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts literally before live deploy is approved.
follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_9: Memo nit N16: animation schemaVersion=2 migrator and forward-compat for animated sprites (multi-frame sprites with explicit frame indexing) deferred to Phase 9. Phase 4's manifest schema has schemaVersion=1; Phase 9 will introduce schemaVersion=2 with animation frames and a migrator that promotes schemaVersion=1 manifests by treating each sprite as a 1-frame animation. The Phase 4.0 acceptance criterion that strict-parse rejects unknown top-level keys means schemaVersion=2 manifests are not silently accepted by Phase 4 atlas-loaders — a build with a schemaVersion=2 manifest must explicitly bump rulesetVersion.
next_phase: Phase 4.A.1 — drift-detection sweep per docs/PHASES.md:248-257. Acceptance criteria summary: uniformIndex relocated from src/sim/run.ts to src/core/prng.ts with focused unit tests (termination, unbiased distribution, n=1 boundary) and 100% line/branch/function coverage on src/core/prng.ts preserved; fdeflate devDependency added to package.json (exact-pinned); .gitattributes created at the repo root with the byte-exact content pinned in addendum B3; tests/build/rules-text.test.ts added asserting LF endings and no UTF-8 BOM on every RULES_FILES entry with pinned error-message format (with skip-when-absent or 4.A.2-pending markers per follow-up N18); vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash scaffolding added with EMPTY_SHA256 fallback and __ATLAS_MISSING__ flag injection via JSON.stringify; deriveRulesetVersion helper exported but not yet called at the define-block site; docs/ARCHITECTURE.md updated to reference Phase 4 frozen contracts including the new src/atlas/** layer and src/atlas/seed.ts atlasSeedToBytes; npm ci && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build all green inside the sandbox with no net-new src/atlas/** recipe code. Phase 4.A.1 is intentionally a no-net-new-atlas drift sweep that gets the foundation ready for Phase 4.A.2 (the actual atlas implementation, which atomically lands assets/atlas.png + assets/atlas.json + the call-site flip per addendum B1).
